Name: 2_crime_kelowna_shootings_police
Representative_Docs: ['One arrested after police raid on West Kelowna ‘problem property’ Published 12:20 pm Monday, February 9, 2026 By Brennan Phillips One person was arrested, and seven others were detained after RCMP executed a search warrant on a West Kelowna property on Feb. 4. The West Kelowna RCMP Crime Reduction Unit (CRU), with the assistance of frontline officers, West Kelowna Drug Section, and the Southeast District Emergency Response Team (SED ERT), worked together to search the Applegreen Court property. According to a Feb. 9 RCMP news release, the warrant was issued as part of a months-long investigation into the “ongoing problem property”, which was the result of repeated complaints from residents in the neighbourhood about the property and the activity associated with it. RCMP seized more than 200 grams of suspected illegal drugs, drug trafficking equipment, ammunition, and weapons. “This investigation is a clear example of our officers listening to the community and taking decisive action where criminal activity is impacting neighbourhood safety,” said Cpl. Bryan Mulrooney, non-commissioned officer-in-charge of the West Kelowna RCMP CRU. “We take residents’ concerns seriously and take enforcement action when warranted. Removing drugs, weapons, and traf', '6 killed in Mississippi mass shooting, suspect charged with murder Authorities have charged a 24-year-old man with murder after six people — including a child — were killed in a series of shootings in northeast Mississippi. Clay County Sheriff Eddie Scott said the victims — four family members related to the suspect and two other adults — were shot at three separate locations late Friday. The youngest victim was a cousin of the suspect. Related video above: Search underway for the killer of Ohio couple "I don\'t know what kind of motive you could have to kill a 7-year-old," Scott said at a press conference Saturday. The suspect was identified as Daricka M. Moore. He faces a first-degree murder charge that could be upgraded to capital murder, Scott said. Moore may face additional murder charges as the investigation proceeds. The shootings took place in the rural community of Cedarbluff, west of the county seat of West Point. Clay County deputies received a call around 5:56 p.m. reporting gunshot victims in the Cedarbluff area; deputies arrived about 7:03 p.m. Investigators allege 24-year-old Daricka Moore shot multiple family members at the first scene and then stole a Ford F-150 belonging to his alleged deceased brother. Name: 3_uss_sanctions_terrorists_strike
Representative_Docs: ["Hegseth, Pentagon stand by explanation of second boat strike (The Center Square) – After a story was published last week claiming that Secretary of War Pete Hegseth ordered a second strike on an alleged Venezuelan drug boat to kill those remaining, both Hegseth and the Pentagon reiterated their defense of the War Department’s actions as completely legal. Pentagon Press Secretary Kingsley Wilson told reporters Tuesday that the U.S. has performed 21 strikes on drug boats in the Western Hemisphere, killing “82 narco-terrorists.” “Our operations in the SOUTHCOM region are lawful under both U.S. and international law, with all actions in compliance with the law of armed conflict,” Wilson said. “These actions have also been approved by the best military and civilian lawyers up and down the chain of command.” Wilson repeatedly described the boats as narco-terrorist vessels at the Pentagon's first press briefing under this administration, underscoring that the terrorist designation allows the Trump administration to respond more forcefully while remaining within the law. Democrats and some Republicans have questioned the legality of the strike, with some calling it a war crime. Name: 4_united_arsenal_villa_chelsea
Representative_Docs: ['Doku, Bobb, Stones, Rodri, Kovacic latest: Man City injury list for West Ham clash By Oliver Thomas | 18 Dec 2025 08:05, Last updated: 18 Dec 2025 08:09 Manchester City will be looking to win their seventh game in a row in all competitions when they welcome West Ham United to the Etihad Stadium for their latest Premier League fixture on Saturday afternoon. The Citizens are in high spirits after securing their spot in the semi-finals of the EFL Cup with a 2-0 home victory over Brentford on Wednesday night, Prior to that triumph, Pep Guardiola’s men beat Crystal Palace by a 3-0 scoreline in the Premier League to climb up to second place in the table and move to within two points of leaders Arsenal. Here, Sports Mole rounds up Man City’s latest injury and suspension news ahead of the contest against the Hammers. Status: Major doubt Type of injury: Hamstring Possible return date: Unknown Oscar Bobb was forced off with an apparent hamstring injury in the EFL Cup win over Brentford and it remains to be seen whether he will be facing a period on the sidelines. Status: Out Type of injury: Leg Possible return date: January 1 (vs. Sunderland) Jeremy Doku has missed Man City’s last two games with a leg injury and Guardiola expects the winger to be sidelined for the next two', "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Sport | Football Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Pressure was piling on Arne Slot after the Reds slumped to ninth in the table, but even without a number of key players, the champions were able to sweep Villa aside to move back up to third. They welcome Real Madrid - Trent Alexander-Arnold, Xabi Alonso, et al - to Anfield in midweek, before they take on Manchester City in a mouth-watering encounter at the weekend. But will they have any of their star names back from injury? Ahead of a big few weeks, here’s all the latest Liverpool injury news and return dates... Alexander Isak has endured a tough start to life on Merseyside. Subbed at half-time in Liverpool’s 5-1 win over Frankfurt in the Champions League, Slot later revealed the £125million summer signing had suffered a minor groin injury. “Alexander had to go off at half-time because he felt his groin a little bit. That's a pity,” said Slot. “I've said many times it is such a difficult balance to find with a player that missed three months.” Isak was not in the squad in the 3-2 loss at Brentford, and he also missed out on the Caraba", "Liverpool injury update: Alexander Isak, Alisson Becker and Jeremie Frimpong latest news and return dates Liverpool returned to winning ways with a 2-0 victory over Aston Villa to end their four-game losing streak in the Premier League. Name: 5_passed away_doris_jane_gloria
Representative_Docs: ['Elaine Jimenez Alvarado Birth: 1967 Death: 2025 Elaine Jimenez Alvarado, age 58, of Santa Ana, California, passed away peacefully on November 11, 2025. Born on August 23, 1967, at St. Joseph Hospital in Orange, CA, Elaine was the beloved daughter of Gilbert and Eustolia Alvarado. Elaine spent her entire life in Santa Ana, where she was a member of the Floral Park community. She dedicated 40 years to the Orange County Register/Freedom Communications Packaging Department, beginning her career as a teenager. Elaine took great pride in her work and loved her job and her coworkers. Elaine was a loving daughter, sister, aunt, and friend. She is survived by her sisters, Annette Tellez and Sandra Rocha; her brothers, Gilbert Alvarado, Mike Alvarado, Albert Alvarado, John Alvarado, and Harvey Alvarado; 11 nephews; 7 nieces; 8 in-laws; and a large extended family who adored her. She was preceded in death by her parents and her older sister, Helga Torres. Known for her generosity, kindness, and beautiful spirit, Elaine touched the hearts of all who knew her. Her gentle presence, loving nature, and unwavering support for her family and friends made her a precious gift to them. She will be deeply missed, and her memor', 'Mary Kuljis Fiamengo Birth: 1934 Death: 2025 Mary Fiamengo passed away peacefully on November 6, 2025. Mary was born on September 20, 1934 in Komiza, Croatia. She was preceded in death by her beloved husband, Pete J. Fiamengo. Mary was a devoted wife, mother, sister, grandmother, great grandmother, aunt and dear friend. She is survived by her daughters, Deborah Dwight and Jennifer (Tim) Lovoy; her grandchildren, Peter Dwight (Cassandra), John Lovoy (Sloane), Ellen (Justin) Ralph and Lauren Dwight (Tim); her great grandchildren, Jordana Koonce, Hank Ralph and Margot Dwight and her sister, Betty Galosic. She was a long standing member of Mary Star of the Sea Church Velike Gospe Society, San Pedro Peninsula Cancer Guild and San Pedro Peninsula Hospital Bookworm Guild. Mary was a woman of grace, elegance and kindness; always extending a gentle word, a thoughtful gesture or a warm embrace. A memorial mass will be held at 10:30 am on Monday, November 17, 2025 at Mary Star of the Sea Catholic Church. In lieu of flowers, Mary requested that a donation in her memory be made to the San Pedro Peninsula Cancer Guild or to Mary Star of the Sea High School. Send Flowers Plant a Tree Send a Meal', 'Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser Birth: 1950 Death: 2025 Linda Sue (Jordan) Schlosser March 16, 1950-December 15, 2025 passed away peacefully surrounded by her family at her home in San Pancho, Mexico. Survived by her husband of 41 years, Phil Schlosser, her mother Gerry, daughter Kelly Noel, son Kevin Noel and wife Jennifer and her grandchildren Trevor and Lena. She was preceded in death by her father, Wayne Henry Jordan and brother Michael Wayne Jordan. Linda was born and raised in San Rafael, CA and graduated from Terra Linda High School. Linda loved sunshine, warm weather and travel. She was an outstanding cook and loved to entertain. Her great adventure was moving to Mexico, building a wonderful home filled with warmth, family, friends and great meals. She loved traveling throughout Mexico and Italy. Her family requests that gifts in lieu of flowers be made in Linda’s name to St. Jude Children’s Research Hospital. Forever deep in my soul, The memory of your love Fills my heart And I am never alone. Honor with Flowers In memory of Linda Sue Plant a Living Memorial In memory of Linda Sue']

Name: 6_passed away_died_anthony_funeral
Representative_Docs: ['Anthony Duarte Birth: 1980 Death: 2025 Anthony Duarte, of La Puente, California, passed away on Wednesday, October 29, 2025, surrounded by love and family. Born on June 9, 1980, in West Covina, California, to Doreen Cardenas and Gildardo Duarte, Anthony was raised with deep love and guidance, and shared a profoundly special bond with his father Robert Cardenas, who helped shape the man he became. Anthony lived a life marked by warmth, creativity, and generosity. His kindness was as constant as his humor, and he approached every day with an open heart and an unwavering sense of purpose. Anthony’s passions reflected his deep belief in connection — through both community and service. He had a profound dedication to civic life, demonstrated by his years of service on the Hacienda La Puente Unified School Board, his time working for a member of Congress, and his contributions to the California Democratic Party. Through these roles, he championed education, representation, and opportunity for others, always guided by empathy and integrity. Anthony cared deeply for his friends and made a lasting impact on everyone lucky enough to know him. Name: 8_education_higher education_curriculum_young minds
Representative_Docs: ['ADC Coalition: Atiku Saved Many Political Actors From Becoming Politically Stranded – Okai Prominent youth advocate and political figure, Comrade Usman Okai Austin, has identified former Vice President and 2023 presidential candidate of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP), Atiku Abubakar, as a major driving force behind efforts to restore Nigeria’s democratic direction ahead of the 2027 general elections. Okai, a chieftain of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), stated this following a closed-door strategic meeting with Atiku, where discussions centred on the urgent need to build a united and formidable opposition capable of challenging the ruling party. The youth leader described the move to form a broad opposition coalition under the ADC platform as timely, especially in view of the prolonged leadership and structural crises rocking the PDP. According to him, Atiku’s intervention has prevented many political actors from becoming politically stranded as a result of the turmoil within their former party. He said, “Today, we are no longer at the mercy of those bent on destroying the PDP from within, because a stronger coalition has emerged due to Atiku’s foresight.” Okai argued that without the ADC-driven alliance, opposition voices would have been largely silenced in Ni', "TEACHING AWARDS TRUST LAUNCHES NEW 'EDUCATION INSIGHTS' EVENT SERIES LONDON, Nov. 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Teaching Awards Trust, supported by Nord Anglia Education, is proud to launch Education Insights, a brand new series of education-focused regional events. Starting in January 2026, the events will connect teachers, school leaders, and local decision-makers with leading voices from sport, technology, business, and politics to explore the defining issues shaping young people's lives and how to unlock their full potential. The series opens on 27 January in Manchester with 'A Focus on Resilience', with former 'Educating Yorkshire' student Musharaf Asghar delivering the keynote address. The programme continues throughout February with four further events exploring critical education themes, including future skills and metacognition (helping young people understand how they learn best). Each event will feature a panel of world-class experts who bring both insights and a passion for young people's development. From BAFTA-winning historian David Olusoga OBE and Adrianne Chapman, Vice Principal at the world-renowned BRIT School, to Baroness Floella Benjamin OM DBE DL, speakers will deliver valuable insights to inspire debate, new thinking, and meani", "TEACHING AWARDS TRUST LAUNCHES NEW 'EDUCATION INSIGHTS' EVENT SERIES LONDON, Nov. 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- The Teaching Awards Trust, supported by Nord Anglia Education, is proud to launch Education Insights, a brand new series of education-focused regional events. Name: 9_family_friendship_family members_friendships
Representative_Docs: ['Asking Eric: Family, friends don’t seem to appreciate time I put into tedious Christmas cookie tradition Dear Eric: My late mother made a special Christmas cookie that required tedious decorating, considering each person’s personal preference for toppings, and giving them as gifts to family and friends. They always had a place of honor on our family Christmas table. When she was no longer able, she gave me the cookie cutter, and my daughters and I took over the family tradition. It takes the three of us several days to make, bake, decorate, package and mail cookies to family members around the country. We enjoy doing it and feel like we’re continuing the fun and festive tradition. However, in recent years, not all of the recipients acknowledge receipt. At some point in their lives, they have all helped Grandma decorate, so they know the time involved. Because of past delivery issues, I track the boxes and inquire if I haven’t heard from them in a couple of days. Last year, one response was “yes we got them.” This feels unappreciative! In the past most would text a picture of their children with the cookies, which we really enjoyed. We rarely see any of them anymore, as they are busy raising their families and don’t live nearby. This seems like a way for us to continue somewhat of a co', 'Asking Eric: Can mom work through tension in son’s relationship with dad so they can celebrate special birthday? Dear Eric: My son is turning 40 on December 22. My husband and I are at a quandary as to how to celebrate him. There have been issues between my husband and him over things from his childhood. We did a special trip for his older brother when he turned 40 and would like to do something special for this son’s 40th as well. Our daughter-in-law has made special plans for him and we are not included. I understand that, but I need some ideas as to how to celebrate this extra special year without rocking the boat. I love my son with all my heart, as I do all my children, and want his 40th birthday to be memorable in a positive way. Any suggestions? – Mom Who Wants to Celebrate Dear Mom: The issues your son and your husband are having may be an obstacle, but they aren’t insurmountable. And the thing that can get them on the path to resolution is the same thing that can get you answers about how to celebrate your son: open communication. It’s not within your purview to try to fix things between them, but you have your own relationship with your son and so I’d encourage you to reach out and tell him what you told me: you want to celebrate him, you know things are difficult, and you’re lookin', 'Asking Eric: After 6 years sober, man is frustrated by his family’s hold on their hurt and distrust Dear Eric: I’ve just turned 40 this past year. The last 15 years I was in a horrible drug addiction. I lied and hurt and did terrible things to a lot of people, especially my family. About eight years ago they officially disowned me. Understandable. I’ve cleaned up and got my act together six years ago. At first, I tried to force my way back into their lives, which all refuted. I lashed out, said horrible things and stopped trying to be in their lives. My mom will stop by on my birthday for 10 minutes or so and drop a card off at Christmas. As for my two older brothers and my father, it’s radio silence. I guess what I’m asking is, what do I do to fix this and fast, as I said I’ve turned 40 this year, my parents are both 70. Time is running out, and I couldn’t imagine living my life without some kind of acceptance from my father. Or knowing he did or does love me. My heart breaks at the thought, but this is a real pickle. How can I fix a problem when the ones I need to fix it with won’t talk to me? Do I just keep ignoring their existence and put on this façade that I don’t care to my wife and 4-year-old son? What picture am I painting to my son, as he’s been guilty by association yo']

Name: 11_ai_artificial intelligence_generative ai_lawsuits
Representative_Docs: ['AI song generator Udio offers brief window for downloads after Universal settlement upsets users Artificial intelligence song generation platform Udio said it would give its frustrated users 48 hours starting Monday to download their songs before the company shifts to a new business model to comply with a legal settlement. The short reprieve comes after Udio on Wednesday said it had settled copyright infringement claims brought by Universal Music, a label with artists including Taylor Swift, Olivia Rodrigo, Drake and Kendrick Lamar. AI companies are now fighting so many copyright lawsuits that a tech industry lobby group, the Chamber of Progress, last week called on President Donald Trump to sign an executive order directing federal attorneys "to intervene in legal cases" to defend the industry\'s practice of building generative AI tools by feeding them on copyrighted works. Citing more than 50 pending federal cases, the group asked for help stopping court fights leading to "potentially company-killing penalties" that threaten AI innovation. But artists have warned that AI tools built on their works also threaten their livelihoods. Name: 14_state government_governor_colorado_state state
Representative_Docs: ['WATCH: Gov. Polis calls out Republicans in State of the State (The Center Square) – Colorado Gov. Jared Polis delivered his last State of the State to a joint session of the Colorado General Assembly on Thursday. In his speech, the Democratic governor called out Republicans, reflected on his past seven years in office, and looked to policy priorities for his last 10 months in office. Since the Trump administration took office one year ago, the Democrat-led Colorado state government has often faced off against the federal government over policies and funding cuts. Polis reflected on that. “Over the past 12 months, it seems like Washington has often been in it to ... make life harder and less affordable, to make Americans feel more fearful, more belligerent and more vulnerable,” Polis said. “In the last month alone, the Trump administration has threatened over $1 billion in funding for Colorado.” As reported by The Center Square, Colorado has filed dozens of lawsuits to challenge federal Republican policies and cuts. Polis promised to keep up those efforts, while calling out Republicans. “We are fighting hard to protect every federal dollar owed to Colorado,” he said. “In partnership with Attorney General [Phil] Weiser, our state has successful', 'WATCH: Gov. Name: 15_county_inmate_prisoners_interior design
Representative_Docs: ['Oakland County debuts 2025 ‘Local Gems’ contest Some shoppers in Oakland County can win between $250 and $1,000 in November as part of the annual Local Gems Sweepstakes. The rules are pretty simple: Visit one or more of the county’s 6,000-plus small retail businesses, take a selfie at the store and submit the entry to the county. A random drawing of selfies submitted online, in person or by mail determines the winner of the three prizes: $1,000, $500, or $250. Every entry must include a photo of the person inside or outside a local small business. The contest gets underway on Saturday, Nov. 1 and continues through Nov. 30. This year’s contest was launched in downtown Pontiac at Designed Mindfully, an interior-design company. Designed Mindfully was among the 2024’s top vote getters, with HobbyTown and AM2PM Barbershop in Southfield; It’s the Little Things in Ortonville; and Vibrant Vizionz in Oak Park. Designed Mindfully owner Deya Marchand said her shop is more than a small store. “We are a space where community, creativity, and intentional living come together,” she said, adding that Local Gems aligns beautifully with her store’s mission “to live and shop with purpose, supporting other small businesses and local artists.” The n', 'Cyber conveyance, in prison Parents can attest to the difference electronics can make on a road trip — for everyone involved. It turns out computer screens have the same effect at quieting the boredom for prisoners in the Nez Perce County Jail. “It has become a very good babysitter,” said NPC Deputy Kael Brink, 38, who was apologetic about the metaphor. “There is a change in inmate behavior.” One of Brink’s jobs is to monitor the use of tablets that are issued to inmates at the 165-capacity Nez Perce County Jail. On Oct. 1, the jail started issuing computer tablets for free to each inmate. These devices not only provide entertainment or educational viewing and listening — they have become how lawyers, loved ones and visitors communicate with inmates, officials say. While the technology is new to Nez Perce County, the Canyon County Jail in Caldwell introduced the tablets two years ago. Immediately, the tablets made the prison population more peaceful. “We saw a decline of 40% for disturbances and fights,” said Canyon County Sheriff’s Deputy Lt. Travis Engle. The rates for fights and disturbances have remained at the new lower level, and may have decreased more, Engle said. Improvements to quality of life Brink', 'Cyber conveyance, in prison Parents can attest to the difference electronics can make on a road trip — for everyone involved. Name: 16_new study_tests_cardiovascular_screening
Representative_Docs: ['Pharming Group receives Complete Response Letter from U.S. FDA for sNDA for Joenja® (leniolisib) in children aged 4 to 11 years with APDS Leiden, the Netherlands, February 1, 2026: Pharming Group (Euronext: PHARM; Nasdaq: PHAR) today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued a Complete Response Letter (CRL) to its supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) for Joenja® (leniolisib), an oral, selective phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta (PI3Kδ) inhibitor, as a treatment for children aged 4 to 11 years with activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta syndrome (APDS), a rare primary immunodeficiency. The FDA raised an issue with the potential for underexposure in lower weight pediatric patients. As a result, the FDA has requested additional pediatric pharmacokinetic data to reassess the proposed pediatric doses and confirm that children in the lower weight dose groups can achieve exposure levels comparable to the approved adult and adolescent regimen. The letter also identified an issue with one of the analytical methods used for production batch testing, and the FDA requested additional data and clarification on this point. We believe we can address the clinical pharmacology and batch testing methodology issues outlined in the letter, and we plan to work closely with the FDA to meet the Agency’s req', 'Pharming Group receives Complete Response Letter from U.S. FDA for sNDA for Joenja® (leniolisib) in children aged 4 to 11 years with APDS Leiden, the Netherlands, February 1, 2026: Pharming Group (Euronext: PHARM; Nasdaq: PHAR) today announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued a Complete Response Letter (CRL) to its supplemental New Drug Application (sNDA) for Joenja® (leniolisib), an oral, selective phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta (PI3Kδ) inhibitor, as a treatment for children aged 4 to 11 years with activated phosphoinositide 3-kinase delta syndrome (APDS), a rare primary immunodeficiency. The FDA raised an issue with the potential for underexposure in lower weight pediatric patients. As a result, the FDA has requested additional pediatric pharmacokinetic data to reassess the proposed pediatric doses and confirm that children in the lower weight dose groups can achieve exposure levels comparable to the approved adult and adolescent regimen. The letter also identified an issue with one of the analytical methods used for production batch testing, and the FDA requested additional data and clarification on this point. We believe we can address the clinical pharmacology and batch testing methodology issues outlined in the letter, and we plan to work closely with the FDA to meet the Agency’s req', 'Disagreement Between Two Kidney Function Tests Predicts Serious Health Problems NEW YORK and HOUSTON, Nov. 7, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- A mismatch between two common tests for kidney function may indicate a higher risk for kidney failure, heart disease, and death, a new study shows. Health care providers for decades have measured blood levels of the molecule creatinine to track the rate at which kidneys filter waste from muscle breakdown in the bloodstream. According to more recent guidelines, levels of cystatin C, a small protein made by all cells in the body, can also be used to measure kidney function. Since these two tests are influenced by different factors — including some related to disease or aging — using both markers together can provide a better measure of kidney function and risk of organ failure than either one alone. Led by NYU Langone Health researchers, the new work reveals that many people, especially those who are sick, often have a large gap between the two readings, which may be a signal of future disease. Specifically, the global study shows that more than a third of hospitalized participants had a cystatin C-based readout of kidney function that was at least 30% lower than one based on their creatinine levels. "Our findings highlight the impor']

Name: 17_fabric_bedroom_staple_items
Representative_Docs: ["Dunelm's 'easy to clean' £25 rug 'you'd never know isn't expensive' Dunelm's 'easy to clean' £25 rug 'you'd never know isn't expensive'(Image: Dunelm) Home shoppers looking for ways to transform their space without spending a lot of money will want to head over to Dunelm. Name: 18_africa_school students_south africa_tourism
Representative_Docs: ['Minnesota high schoolers prepare for once-in-lifetime learning trip to Africa ASHBY, Minn. — There are field trips and there are field trips. Students at Ashby High School are busy raising money and getting ready to pack their bags for a two-week learning experience in South Africa. ADVERTISEMENT When it is below zero, the main street in Ashby, Minnesota, is quiet. Fewer than 500 people call this Grant County town home, but lately the town is busy rallying around a group of high school students. In the basement of Peace Grue Lutheran in the middle of town, five high school students join veteran church quilters in tying quilts that the students will take on their trip to South Africa. "There is a reason the church ladies do this and not the guys," joked Kevin Fick, the girls\' weightlifting coach and organizer of the trip, of tying quilts the students will take with them as gifts. Five girls, along with Fick and four adult chaperones, will make the trip to Eastern Cape, South Africa, from Feb. 28 through March 10. Not as tourists, but to learn about conservation on another continent. "Not a lot of kids get to do this," said student Hadley Hokanson, student. They will work a sheep ranch, fish for sharks, harvest their own food, camp in the bushes and more. Representative_Docs: ['Budget 2026: FM Proposes Raising Electronics Manufacturing Outlay To Rs 40,000 cr In FY27 Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman on Sunday proposed to increase the outlay on electronics manufacturing to Rs 40,000 crore in the 2026-27 fiscal year. Presenting the Union Budget for 2026-27, she said high-tech tool rooms will be established at two locations to give push to capital goods manufacturing. Sitharaman also proposed a scheme for container manufacturing to create globally competitive ecosystem. Three dedicated chemical parks will also be set up to enhance domestic production, reduce import dependency, she added. The move comes amid a massive thrust by the government on increasing electronics manufacturing in the country. The mobile manufacturing segment witnessed a nearly 30-fold increase in production value, rising from Rs 18,000 crore in FY15 to Rs 5.45 lakh crore in FY25. iPhone exports from India have hit Rs 2.03 lakh crore in 2025, which is almost double that of Rs 1.1 lakh crore Apple exported in the calendar year 2024. Mobile phone production in the country is expected to reach about Rs 6.76 lakh crore, comprising exports over USD 30 billion, or about Rs 2.7 lakh crore, by the end of the current fiscal year. Representative_Docs: ["Egypt unveils the world's largest museum dedicated to a single civilization CAIRO (AP) — Egypt is inaugurating on Saturday the Grand Egyptian Museum, two decades in the making, with the aim of drawing more visitors to revitalize the country's tourist industry. Located just outside Cairo on the Giza Plateau which also includes the three pyramids and the Sphinx, the museum will be the world’s largest dedicated to a single civilization. It will display over 50,000 artifacts detailing life in ancient Egypt. World leaders, including monarchs, heads of states and governments, are set to attend the grand opening ceremony, according to the Egyptian presidency which touted the museum as “an exceptional event in the history of human culture and civilization.” The museum is one of the megaprojects championed by President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi since he took office in 2014. He has embarked on massive investments in infrastructure with the aim of reviving an economy weakened by decades of stagnation and battered by the unrest that followed the 2011 Arab Spring uprising. Preparations for the grand reveal have been shrouded in secrecy. The museum, which was opened for limited visits in recent years, has been closed for the past two weeks. Name: 23_tennis_boxing_heavyweight_match
Representative_Docs: ['Sabalenka serves her way past Paolini at WTA Finals; Gauff hits 17 double-faults in loss to Pegula RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — Top-ranked Aryna Sabalenka put on a dominant serving display in her opening match at the WTA finals, producing 10 aces in a 6-3, 6-1 win over No. 8-ranked Jasmine Paolini on Sunday. Sabalenka served four aces in the final game of the first set alone and overall put 82.7% of her first serves in play. The match, which was Sabalenka’s 500th at the WTA level, lasted 70 minutes. “I was focused, I was calm and it felt like everything was in control,” Sabalenka said. On Saturday, Sabalenka was presented with a trophy for finishing the year at No. 1. “I’m super happy and super proud of the work that has been done and that things are working and I’m getting better and better every day,” she said. “And I just hope that we’re going to keep doing what we’re doing and hopefully we can stay there.” The season-ending tournament for the year’s top eight players starts with two four-woman groups of round-robin play. The top two finishers in each group advance to the semifinals. “I take this tournament as a regular tournament that I have to win five matches if I want to win the title,” Sabalenka said. “So I’m just trying to bring my best tennis and fight for every point.”', 'Retiring Wawrinka winds back the clock with gutsy win Stan Wawrinka\'s farewell tour has started with a gutsy win, the three-time grand slam champion beating world No.29 Arthur Rinderknech in a three-set thriller at the United Cup. Hold tight - we’re checking permissions before loading more content Wawrinka announced last month that 2026 will be his final year on tour, but he warned he wasn\'t planning to go quietly. World No.11 Belinda Bencic had given Switzerland a 1-0 lead earlier in the day by beating Leolia Jeanjean 6-2 6-4 in 91 minutes. After an epic match that lasted three hours and 18 minutes, Wawrinka joked he had hoped for a quicker contest to begin 2026. "But no, this is why I keep playing," Wawrinka said. "I\'m passionate about the game. I\'m always going to push my own limit. I\'ve said I\'ve been working hard this off-season. "Even if it\'s my last year, I don\'t just want to play to play. I want to play to compete and try to win. "[Anthony] is a serious heavyweight," Callen said, "It\'s actually physically dangerous for Jake Paul." Name: 24_immigration_britain_immigrants_immigrant
Representative_Docs: ['‘UK public have tougher stance on immigration and diversity than people in US’ People in the UK are less likely than their US counterparts to feel diversity strengthens society and more likely to have tougher attitudes on immigration, according to research. The findings are said to show that a common narrative about Britain being more socially liberal than America does not ring true across all issues. The National Centre for Social Research also found Reform UK voters in Britain were more likely than supporters of President Donald Trump in the US to say immigrants living in their country without permission should not be allowed to stay. While seven in 10 supporters of Mr Trump felt this way, the figure was almost eight in 10 (79%) among Reform followers. The researchers said they wanted to “better understand how public attitudes in the UK compare with those in the US, and the extent of polarisation in the two countries”. Their survey found 42% of people in the UK think there should be legal efforts to deport immigrants living here without permission, compared with just a third (33%) in the US. While almost two-thirds (64%) of respondents in the US felt diversity strengthens society, only around half (49%) of the UK public agreed. Name: 25_hockey team_hockey_olympics_olympic
Representative_Docs: ['Heartbreak for Canada, Sidney Crosby in Olympic hockey final MILAN -- For a nation that measures itself in hockey gold, Sunday\'s heartbreaking 2-1 defeat to the United States in overtime was more than a lost final. It was a symbolic shift in a rivalry Canada has long considered its own to win. Canada, owners of nine Olympic men\'s hockey titles to the Americans\' three, is a country whose national identity is intertwined with the sport. This one will sting. "Losing hurts, doesn\'t matter who it\'s to," Canada\'s coach Jon Cooper said. "I look at this game, I\'m so proud of what went on. I\'m proud of our country. I\'m proud of our players and proud of our team. "At the end, you\'re going to read in a book that in 2026 USA won the gold in Olympic hockey and Canada won the silver. I think in the grand scheme, the big winner of this tournament was ice hockey." SHADOW OF CROSBY\'S ABSENCE Back home, bleary-eyed Canadians had nursed beers before breakfast, bars opening as early as 5 a.m. on the West Coast as a nation gathered in hope. Instead, they watched the U.S. celebrating, and Canada left to swallow a rare heartbreak on hockey\'s biggest stage, and on the heels of Canada\'s women\'s team losing gold to the U.S. in overtime. The absence of captain Sidney', 'U.S. Women’s Hockey Team declines Trump invite to State of the Union The U.S. Women’s Hockey Team declined an invite to President Donald Trump‘s State of the Union address on Monday, shortly after a clip of Trump making light of the women’s team made the rounds. Both American squads won gold at the 2026 Winter Olympics in Milan, taking down the Canadian team in their respective finals. On Sunday, Trump invited the men’s team to his annual speech and joked about the inclusion of the women’s team. “I must tell you, we’re going to have to bring the women’s team, you do know that,” Trump said, adding that he “probably would be impeached” if he didn’t. Everything about this is so genuinely gross but the “I guess I have to invite the women’s team too or else I’ll get impeached” is fucking crazy. Britta Curl is that your boy? Abbey Murphy you cool with this? pic.twitter.com/dTgpBF7hda — Maya🇵🇸🇱🇧 (@ilyasamsHOEnov) February 23, 2026 Related Trump celebrated the men’s hockey win on social media, lauding the team on Truth Social after their overtime victory. “Congratulations to our great U.S.A. Ice Hockey team. THEY WON THE GOLD. WOW!” he wrote. When the women’s team won just days earlier, Trump remained silent. Start your day with essential news from Salon. On Sunday, Trump invited the men’s team to his annual speech and joked about the inclusion of the women’s team. “I must tell you, we’re going to have to bring the women’s team, you do know that,” Trump said, adding that he “probably would be impeached” if he didn’t. Trump celebrated the men’s hockey win on social media, lauding the team on Truth Social after their overtime victory. “Congratulations to our great U.S.A. Ice Hockey team. THEY WON THE GOLD. WOW!” he wrote. When the women’s team won just days earlier, Trump remained silent. Start your day with essential news from Salon. Sign up for our free morning newsletter, Crash Course. On Monday, the women’s team shared in a statement that they would not be attending the State of the Union. The statement made no mention of Trump’s comments, citing “academic and professional commitments” for the athletes’ decision. “We']

Name: 26_harry_prince_royal_meghan markle
Representative_Docs: ["Prince Harry's visa row back in spotlight as Andrew asked to testify in US Prince Harry’s visa row has been brought back into the spotlight as a result of the latest news that Andrew Mountbatten Windsor has been asked to appear before the US Congress for questioning over his links to convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. On Thursday evening, it was revealed that Andrew, who has recently been stripped of all his royal titles as a result of scandals surrounding his past friendship with Epstein, has now been asked to give evidence in the US. Andrew has always denied any allegations made against him. While this has led to mixed public reaction, one public figure has said that if this is the case for Andrew, Prince Harry should also be asked to testify before Congress in the US about his visa. This is because over recent years, think tank group The Heritage Foundation have repeatedly asked for Harry’s US visa to be made public to see if he lied about his past drug use. This is because Harry has lived in America since 2020, but revealed in his 2023 memoir that he took drugs as a teenager. On Wednesday, the Duke of Sussex was spotted taking part in the St. Regis World Snow Polo Championship in Aspen, Colorado, just hours after he was spotted skiing with close friend Nacho Figueras. The Prince, 41, reportedly was taking part in the polo tournament to stand in for a friend who couldn’t make it. He is believed to have been on the Aspen Valley team. Harry would have most likely been delighted to take part in the event, having played polo for a number of years. Buckingham Palace made the announcement on Thursday after weeks of Andrew dominating the headlines as a result of scandals involving his past friendship with the late convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. While Andrew has always denied all accusations against him, the King’s brother was aware of how he was being a distraction to the monarchy, and on October 17 said he would no longer use his royal titles. However, King Charles took this a step further by formally removing them from him - a move that many did not see coming. While the King’s actions have been praised, some believe the monarch, 76, took too long to take action, especially over evicting the former prince from his Crown Estate mansion, however Queen Elizabeth's former press secretary Ailsa Anderson has now explained why. Representative_Docs: ['The FIFO CEO: Rail Loop boss commutes from Queensland Add articles to your saved list and come back to them any time. The $900,000-a-year executive responsible for Melbourne’s most expensive infrastructure project commutes from Brisbane and is paid additional expenses to attend unscheduled meetings. Freedom of information documents released to The Age reveal the extraordinary arrangements to enable Suburban Rail Loop Authority chief executive Frankie Carroll to maintain a residence in Brisbane, fly in and out of his Big Build job, and bill taxpayers for flight costs when he is “recalled to duty”. Suburban Rail Loop Authority chief executive Frankie Carroll. This is an employment term used when staff are asked to work or return to the office outside their regular work hours. Carroll, who is on a remuneration package of between $900,000 and $909,999, has led the Allan government’s signature infrastructure project since 2021. This includes superannuation and accrued leave and other entitlements. While Carroll meets the cost of his regular commute himself, the documents show that taxpayers were charged when Carroll flew to Melbourne, where the rail loop authority is headquartered, to attend a conference. In another example of travel costs', '‘We’re in it for the fight’: State school teachers rally outside Parliament Add articles to your saved list and come back to them any time. Anyone who claims DWP benefits should be in line for an increase in the next financial year, as the chancellor laid out plans to help pensioners, disabled people, parents, low earners, carers and unemployed people keep up with rising costs. Name: 30_tvs_monitor_display_screen
Representative_Docs: ['Samsung OLED 48S95H: New 48-inch and 165 Hz OLED TV revealed ahead of schedule with HDR10 Plus Advanced support Samsung has already teased plenty of devices on the eve of CES 2026. However, the trade show has decided to lift the lid on one of Samsung\'s forthcoming OLED Smart TVs. For context, Samsung listed numerous unreleased Smart TVs on its Parts Database over a month ago. Of those, CES has announced that the S95H is a 2026 Honoree in its Gaming & eSports category. Specifically, the trade show has signalled out a 48-inch variant that was missing from November\'s leak. Incidentally, last year\'s S95F only went as small as 55-inches (curr. $1,997 on Amazon). According to the listing, the S95H offers a 165 Hz refresh rate with VRR technology supported. Assuming a 4K resolution from the S95F series and the usage of HDMI 2.1 ports, a 165 Hz refresh rate would require the usage of Display Stream Compression (DSC) to output at its peak resolution and refresh rate simultaneously. Moreover, the new 48-inch Smart TV will support Samsung\'s new HDR10 Plus Advanced format. Pricing and availability are unknown for now; the full description provided by Samsung and CES is as follows: The Samsung OLED (S95H) is the world\'s most advanced 48" OLED TV for high-end gaming. Combining unparalleled brightness and', 'Luckfox 16-inch 4K 120Hz portable touch monitor offers 100% sRGB coverage The segment of portable monitors continues to grow, with many models available on Amazon at relatively affordable prices, typically between $100 and $150. These screens can be useful for productivity on business trips or while traveling. But portable monitors that combine a high resolution with a fast refresh rate remain uncommon in the mid-range segment. Luckfox wants to address this gap with its new 16" 4K Touch Monitor. The 16-inch display features a 3840 × 2400 (WQUXGA) IPS panel with 350 nits brightness and a 1200:1 contrast ratio. It uses optical bonding technology with 6H-hardness toughened glass and a matte finish to minimize ambient reflections. The 16:10 aspect ratio provides more vertical workspace than the standard 16:9 format. It also supports a 120Hz refresh rate, which is an upgrade over the common 60Hz panels found in many portable monitors, such as the Auzai ME16Z01 or the Arzopa A1M; response times aren\'t disclosed, though. As for connectivity, the product integrates two USB Type-C ports and a Mini HDMI input. Both USB-C ports support audio, video, and power delivery, meaning that only a single USB-C cable is required when connected to a compatible smartphone, tab', "World's largest monitor company designed a 4K 48-inch OLED dual-sided smart gaming display with metal feet, a magnetic power socket, and an Alienware-inspired sound system - but who will actually use it? Sign up for breaking news, reviews, opinion, top tech deals, and more. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful GP TV has unveiled what it calls the world’s first dual-sided 48-inch 4K OLED television, designed for gaming and sports viewing. The display is intended to deliver immersive experiences on both sides simultaneously, with built-in audio and dynamic lighting effects. TPV Technology Group, the company behind GP TV, claims the screen targets premium users who value high-end visual fidelity, as well as the public sector and government institutions. This design feels unusual because most monitors or televisions sit against a wall or on a desk, making a rear-facing display largely unnecessary. Its relevance only emerges if the screen stands in the center of a room, similar to display setups commonly seen in shopping malls - and even then, deploying such a device in public spaces would increase the risk of physical damage, leaving the practical use case for this product uncertain. Name: 31_legislative_legislators_school board_representatives
Representative_Docs: ['Working groups provide House Education Committee with updates and recommendations The Vermont House of Representatives Committee on Education is wasting no time getting up to speed with the status of several working groups designed to deliver recommendations on Vermont’s education system. In the first days of the legislative session, the committee heard updates from the Agency of Education, the State Aid for School Construction Working Group, and the Commission on the Future of Public Education in Vermont. AOE Secretary Zoie Saunders joined the committee Tuesday afternoon to share how the agency has been working to reorganize its structure and develop a strategic plan. “We recognize that there are increasing demands on the (AOE) for transformative leadership and also direction-setting to elevate academic achievement and improve operational efficiency,” Saunders said. She said that a series of “Listen and Learn” tours held last year indicated a need for stronger academic leadership, training and support. Currently, Saunders and her deputy secretary oversee eight separate divisions, including federal education support programs, education quality, student support services, student pathways division, data management and analysis division, legal, finance, and policy', 'Senate looks at barring sale of lands BOISE — The Senate State Affairs Committee voted to introduce a proposed amendment to the Idaho State Constitution that would bar the state from selling future public land acquisitions from the federal government. Sen. Ben Adams, R-Nampa, presented his proposal Wednesday following a public outreach campaign to gauge Idahoans’ concerns on the issue of public lands and receive feedback on the language within his amendment. With adjustments now implemented, Adams said the original intent remains: ensuring public lands continue to belong to Idahoans. “I wanted feedback,” Adams said. “I wanted a lot of feedback because it dealt with, and deals with, a very personal matter for every Idahoan, and that is our public land here in Idaho.” In order to have an amendment added to the Idaho State Constitution, it would require an affirmative vote from two-thirds of both the Idaho House of Representatives and the Idaho Senate. If approved by the legislature this session, it will be on the ballot for Idaho voters in the 2026 election, where it requires a simple majority to pass. The draft amendment states that public lands acquired after the amendment’s passage are to be held in a trust as public', 'Senate looks at barring sale of lands BOISE — The Senate State Affairs Committee voted to introduce a proposed amendment to the Idaho State Constitution that would bar the state from selling future public land acquisitions from the federal government. Representative_Docs: ['High-end Tampa home remodeling firm expanding into Orlando, plans 70 jobs Loading Revive Design and Renovation, a Tampa-based home remodeling company, is expanding into the Orlando market with a new $750,000 showroom and 70 employees. The new Revive showroom will be at 601 S. New York Ave. in Winter Park. The 5,000-square-foot shop is currently under construction and expected to open this spring. (The property is the former home of Ethos Vegan Kitchen, a restaurant that closed in 2024.) Justin Caballero, Revive’s founder and president, says in a statement that the company is investing in the Orlando market because it “sees the same dynamics that fueled our success in Tampa, rapid growth, strong home values, and a high concentration of homes ready for thoughtful, award-winning, high-end renovations.” The company says it expects to profit from a strong demand for remodels in Orlando’s Windemere neighborhood and Winter Park. Revive Design and Renovation’s Tampa showroom and design center is at 3102 W. Kennedy Blvd. Founded in 2020, it serves clients both in the Tampa Bay and Sarasota markets, with, a spokesperson says, its Sarasota work focused on high-end beach front homes and in Lakewood Ranch. The spokesperson says in an email that the Tampa operation su', 'Builder buys 53 townhouse sites in St. Pete waterfront development for $12M Loading Mattamy Homes has bought 53 townhouse sites in St. Petersburg’s Amara Bay Residences & Marina master-planned community currently under construction. The sites at 12000 Gandy Blvd. sold for $12.64 million. Collier, which brokered the deal and announced the sale, says in a statement that the lots are fully entitled and approved as part of the master-planned community. Construction of the townhouses is expected to begin in coming weeks with sales starting in the summer. Mattamy Homes, Collier says in a statement, will develop two townhome designs with two stories of living space. They will range from 2,371 square feet to 2,470 square feet with three to four bedrooms, two-and-a-half baths and two-car garages. Pricing is expected to start in the $700,000s. Amara Bay is a 39-acre mixed-use development just as you come into Pinellas County from the Gandy Bridge. It is being developed by the Miami firm Key Development, which sold the lots to Mattamy Homes. Plans for the master-planned community call for 415 luxury apartments, a 150-slip marina, an 8,000-square-foot restaurant and 3,000 feet of walkable waterfront. Representative_Docs: ['AP Sports SummaryBrief at 3:08 p.m. EST MLB pitchers Emmanuel Clase and Luis Ortiz charged with taking bribes to rig pitches for bettors NEW YORK (AP) — Two Major League Baseball pitchers have been indicted on charges they took bribes to give sports bettors advance notice of the types of pitches they’d throw and intentionally tossed balls instead of strikes to ensure successful bets. Emmanuel Clase and Luis Ortiz both pitched for the Cleveland Guardians. They have been on non-disciplinary paid leave since July while Major League Baseball investigated what it said was unusually high in-game betting activity when they pitched. A lawyer for Ortiz declined to comment when contacted by The Associated Press and a lawyer for Clase did not immediately return messages seeking comment. Paul Tagliabue, NFL commissioner of 17 years who led an era of riches and expansion, dies at 84 NEW YORK (AP) — Paul Tagliabue, who helped bring labor peace and riches to the NFL during his 17 years as commissioner but was criticized for not taking stronger action on concussions, died Sunday from heart failure at 84 years old. NFL spokesman Brian McCarthy said Tagliabue’s family informed the league of his death in Chevy Chase, Maryland. Vásquez, File) NORTH PORT, Fla. — Atlanta Braves right-handed starter Spencer Schwellenbach had surgery Wednesday to clean up “loose bodies” in his elbow, and rotation candidate Hurston Waldrep is scheduled to undergo a similar procedure next week. While the Braves didn’t reveal a timeline for when the pitchers could be available, manager Walt Weiss told reporters Wednesday that he is hopeful that both will be able to pitch this season. Schwellenbach was placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 10, at the start of spring training, because of inflammation in his right elbow. The 25-year-old was 7-4 with a 3.08 ERA in 17 starts last year before missing the final three months of the season because of a broken right elbow. Waldrep, the Braves’ first-round pick out of Florida in the 2023 amateur draft, experienced discomfort after throwing batting practice over the weekend. An MRI showed no ligament damage for the pitcher who turns 24 on March 1. Name: 35_customs enforcement_immigration_citizens_detained
Representative_Docs: ["California sets up a showdown with Washington by reissuing licenses to migrant truckers Trucks were parked in Bakersfield, California, in November because of a lack of drivers after a crackdown on immigrant drivers. California has delayed its cancellation of thousands of commercial driver's licenses held by migrants, setting it up for another showdown with Washington. The Department of Motor Vehicles announced on Tuesday that the 17,000 migrant truck drivers whose licenses had been revoked can now keep them for 60 more days, which could enable the drivers to retake tests and do whatever is necessary to remain legal. Representative_Docs: ['States scramble to send full SNAP food benefits to millions of people after shutdown ends The US government shutdown finally ended on November 12 after 43 days of budget paralysis – the longest in the nation’s history. Federal operations are resuming, but key issues remain unresolved and tensions in Washington persist. With the longest U.S. government shutdown over, state officials said Thursday they were working quickly to get full SNAP food benefits to millions of people who made do with little to no assistance for the past couple of weeks. Name: 37_petrol_fuel_vehicles_singapore
Representative_Docs: ['Europe Leads the Charge on Aviation Sustainability with SAF Mandates and Innovation Published on November 2, 2025 Europe is steadily advancing toward greener skies, with the aviation industry accelerating its shift towards sustainability. The key driver? Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). SAF is a biofuel made from renewable resources that reduces carbon emissions and is integral to achieving Europe’s ambitious carbon-neutral goals. With recent regulations, such as the EU’s ReFuelEU Aviation mandate, the aviation industry is witnessing a transformative shift in fuel consumption.What Are SAF Mandates?Advertisement Since January 2025, European airports have been mandated to blend a minimum of 2% SAF with conventional jet fuel. This percentage will increase incrementally, reaching 6% by 2030 and 70% by 2050. These regulations are part of the European Union’s broader strategy to decarbonize the aviation sector. ReFuelEU Aviation, an EU regulation first drafted in 2021, aims to make SAF the primary fuel for aircraft departing from European airports, bolstering sustainable aviation while fostering innovation and growth in the biofuel sector.The Role of SAF in DecarbonizationAdvertisement The EU’s SAF mandate plays a critical role in achieving the aviation industry’s carbo', 'Singapore port sets new performance records in 2025 Singapore (VNA) – Data released by the Maritime and Port Authority of Singapore (MPA) show that both vessel arrival tonnage and container throughput reached record highs in 2025, supported by stable global trade conditions and strong fuel demand. An MPA press release issued on January 13 said total vessel arrival tonnage at the Port of Singapore last year hit an all-time high of 3.22 billion tonnes, up 3.5% compared with 2024. At the same time, container throughput rose sharply by 8.6% year-on-year to 44.66 million TEUs (twenty-foot equivalent units). The authority also reported that marine fuel sales set a new record at 56.77 million tonnes, up 3.4% from 2024. Notably, the use of alternative marine fuels continued to expand, reaching 1.95 million tonnes in 2025, compared with 1.35 million tonnes a year earlier. Over the past year, Singapore also garnered a series of prestigious international accolades, ranging from being named the world’s leading container port in DNV-Menon’s inaugural “Top Container Ports of the World” report, to being honoured as the Best Global Seaport for the fourth time and Best Seaport in Asia for the 37th time at the 2025 Supply Chain Asia Awards. Singapore', 'Giving stalling car industry a break on petrol-engine ban could be a mistake A law banning petrol and diesel engines in new cars by 2035, supposed to be one of the main planks of Europe’s ambitious green agenda, has become the latest front in a Brussels tug of war between climate and industrial policy. The law is part of the European Union’s efforts to become climate neutral by the middle of the century, by phasing out polluting cars and encouraging people to switch to greener electric vehicles (EVs). You will still be able to buy second-hand petrol or diesel cars after 2035, but new vehicles coming off the manufacturing lines will have to be battery-powered. The combustion engine ban, which was approved by the European Parliament and national governments two years ago, is in the sights of the stalling car industry. Volkswagen, BMW and other big German carmakers, which Berlin has rowed in behind, are looking for leeway on the deadline and specific carveouts. The fight cuts across several big questions policymakers are wrestling with: climate targets, the level of regulation from Brussels, Europe’s sluggish economy and the threat of China eating the EU’s lunch. European Commission president Ursula von der Leyen, whose “green deal” reforms introduced the 2035']

Name: 38_rugby_zealand_cooper_williams
Representative_Docs: ['With early tests now passed, Ireland go in search of World Cup progression Ireland’s Jaden Umeh and head coach Colin O\'Brien. Pic: Inpho Two wins and a draw earned Colin O\'Brien\'s Republic of Ireland under-17s top spot in Group J and a Round of 32 clash with Canada at the World Cup in Qatar. So what will the knockout phase bring that the group stage didn\'t? O\'Brien spoke post-Paraguay of how "cup football" awaits following their successful navigation of the group stage. That win-or-go-home pressure also awaits. Head coach O\'Brien acknowledged the experience collected thus far - be that the occasion, the Doha heat, officials, or opposition tactics. For the Cork native, it has given his players plenty of knowledge in terms of how they best go about the Round of 32 and possibly beyond. "We\'ve gathered so much info from the tournament, how it works, everything around it," O\'Brien said. "The supporters, some of the officials. So the players have a lot of information now. "They\'re probably being educated now on how to deal with certain things. Early in the games, they might get frustrated with certain things. It\'s our job as a staff to say: \'Right, this is what you might have to do in a certain situation. This is what\'s happening with a player, or happening wit', 'One of the most exciting cross-code talents is ready to shine in green and gold One of the region\'s best dual-code prospects has received his biggest call-up yet. Login or signup to continue reading Cooper Williams has been called into the Australia under 16s rugby union team, having had a breakout season with NSW Waratahs, that featured in the grand final win over Queensland in the Super Rugby U16 championship. But he\'s also starred in the 13-man code, having worn the black and white of the Western Suburbs Magpies in the Harold Matthews Cup. The Year 11 St Stanislaus\' College student from Canowindra said he was in "disbelief" when he found out it was called into the national side. "I was shocked, I just couldn\'t believe it," the Canowindra Pythons junior said. "I\'ve always dreamed about it, making really high rep sides and stuff. But this, I\'m so grateful for people around me, like my family. "It\'s just a great moment for me. My dad told me [over the phone that I was named] and my Nan was in the background and I could hear her clapping. "There\'s so much disbelief, for out of the 1000s and 1000s of boys that play football and rugby union in Australia, I\'m one of the 25 boys to make the side. Name: 39_central bank_federal reserve_florence_meeting
Representative_Docs: ['Florence Press Conference Confirms ECB Remains Comfortable in Its Good Place Even a change of scenery has done nothing to change the European Central Bank’s current wait-and-see stance. Florence, where the ECB met today for its only annual meeting outside of Frankfurt, is apparently also this ’good place’, the central bank’s well-known pseudonym for comfortably keeping interest rates on hold. ECB President Christine Lagarde even called it a ‘magnificent’ place – Florence, that is, not rates. The comments at the press conference confirmed once again that the ECB has no intention to leave its ‘good place’ any time soon. We had feared a rather uneventful press conference. What should we say? We hate to be right… Anyhow, here are a few interesting remarks made by Lagarde: On the ECB’s risk assessment, the introductory statement had the most remarkable change compared with September. Last month, the ECB had still talked about risks to the growth outlook that had become “more balanced”; today, it refrained from qualifying any balance of risk. Instead, the central bank had a somewhat more extensive list of upside and downside risks to the growth outlook. Lagarde even said that some of the downside risks had abated. Name: 40_santa_christmas_holiday season_christmas eve
Representative_Docs: ['Celebrating Christmas in and around Larnaca The city, the suburbs and the villages of Larnaca all get into the festive mood this season as dozens of Christmas events pop up. All around the Larnaca region, markets, workshops and performances will share magical moments for visitors. The SanTa Lefkara Christmas Village is on until January 6 with artists’ stalls, entertainment, craft workshops, children’s activities and concerts taking over the village centre. As one of the deputy ministry of tourism’s eight Christmas Villages, Lefkara is dressed in full holiday glam with glimmering string lights, Christmas trees and a rich, weekly entertainment programme. On December’s first weekend coming up, Larnaca region’s biggest fair returns. The Traditional Christmas Market makes its anticipated annual comeback at Forest Beach, Dekeleia Road, bringing over 200 stalls selling a wide range of food, craft and handmade items from handbags to sweets, candles, baked goods and hundreds of other unique items. The market will include Santa visits, live radio links, hot drinks and food stands. The Livadia Christmas Programme will also bring holiday cheer on December 6, 14, 20 and 28 as the community square fills with music and dance beats, the fam', 'Want an ‘ezbacke uvin’ or ‘mot cantrl’ for Christmas? Write to Santa The time has come again for both the young and old to send in their letters to Santa. This has become one of my favorite Christmas traditions. The letters to Santa we publish each December are often heartwarming and occasionally heartbreaking. They are a reminder of what makes Christmas magical. They are an amusing break from the sometimes jarring news we must print in the paper. And, hey, they might even be beneficial to your brain’s elasticity. It takes some creativity on the reader’s part to figure out just what the little kiddies are asking for, since we publish the letters verbatim. Try to decipher these phrases from previous Santa letters: I’m looking forward to the, umm, creative writing in this year’s batch of letters. We will publish Santa letters from children and adults in north central Idaho and southeastern Washington. The letters can be just a few words if you have a short wish list, but please try to write no more than 300 words. Be sure to include the author’s name, age and town of residence. The letters can be emailed to city@lmtribune.com, sent in the mail to Letters to Santa, Lewiston Tribune, 505 Capital St., Lewiston, ID 83501, or dropped off at our office. Name: 41_surgical_hospital_memorial hospital_patients
Representative_Docs: ['VIDEO: $5.5M robotic surgery system arrives at Surrey Memorial Hospital Published 12:10 pm Tuesday, January 27, 2026 By Anna Burns The da Vinci Xi System has been unpacked and calibrated and is being held in a temporary location until its permanent location is finished. Fraser Health said that it expects it to “go-live” later in the spring. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Health care workers pose in front of the da Vinci Xi System. (Fraser Health/ Contributed to Surrey Now-Leader) Surrey Memorial Hospital is one step closer to providing robotic-assisted surgery with the arrival of the da Vinci Xi Robotic Surgery system. It is expected to be used in surgeries in the spring. “With the aid of the da Vinci Xi system, we can expand minimally invasive thoracic and foregut surgery, and we can do operations with greater precision and accuracy,” says Dr Ahmad Ashrafi, regional division head and chief of thoracic surgery for Fraser Health. “Patients may experience quicker recovery times, and we can carry out more complex procedures right here in Surrey, using minimally invasive techniques.
